rationale: Ground truth has no substantive concerns (just an 'lgtm' approval), and both candidates correctly reach APPROVE with grounded, diff-consistent verification of the rename's completeness (repo-wide grep counts match the diff's 9 sites exactly). Neither fabricates issues. X edges ahead slightly by adding a genuine non-blocking insight (tests mock the attribute directly and won't catch future upstream drift) and presenting findings more concisely, whereas Y's repetitive per-hunk '[validated]' formatting adds length without added value.
